request.description:
StartFragment

## Response Documentation

### Endpoint Tested

`GET https://dummyjson.com/products/1`

### Status Code

`200 OK`

### Description

This endpoint retrieves detailed information about a specific product.  
The response returns a JSON object containing product metadata such as identification, pricing, category, stock availability, dimensions, reviews, and images.

---

## Response Structure

### Product Information

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | Integer | Unique identifier of the product |
| title | String | Name of the product |
| description | String | Detailed description of the product |
| category | String | Product category |
| price | Number | Price of the product |
| discountPercentage | Number | Discount applied to the product |
| rating | Number | Average product rating |
| stock | Integer | Number of products available in inventory |
| tags | Array | Keywords associated with the product |
| brand | String | Brand name of the product |
| sku | String | Stock Keeping Unit used for product tracking |
| weight | Number | Product weight |

---

## Product Dimensions

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| width | Number | Product width |
| height | Number | Product height |
| depth | Number | Product depth |

---

## Shipping and Warranty Information

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| warrantyInformation | String | Warranty period for the product |
| shippingInformation | String | Estimated shipping time |
| availabilityStatus | String | Product availability status |

---

## Customer Reviews

The response includes an array of customer reviews.

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |

exec: pm.test("Products array is not empty", function () {

request.description:
StartFragment

### Endpoint

GET {{base_url}}/products/search?q={{search_query}}

### Status Code

200 OK

### Response

The API returns a JSON object containing a **products array** with products that match the search query. Each product includes details such as id, title, description, category, price, rating, stock, and brand.

### Additional Fields

- **total** – total number of products matching the search query
    
- **skip** – number of products skipped (pagination)
    
- **limit** – maximum number of products returned
    

### Observation

The search endpoint successfully returned products related to the search query. The response also includes pagination fields to control the number of results returned.

EndFragment

### Scenario: Error Handling – Invalid Product ID

In this test, the goal is to verify how the API behaves when a **non-existing product ID** is requested.

**The request sent was:**

GET {{base_url}}/products/{{invalid_id}}

StartFragmentAn invalid ID (for example `0` or a very large number) was used to simulate a situation where the requested product does not exist in the database.EndFragment  

StartFragment

### Expected Behaviour

When a product ID does not exist, the API should:

- Return a **404 Not Found** status code
    
- Provide an **error message explaining the issue**

### Endpoint

POST {{base_url}}/products/add

### Description

This endpoint creates a new product by sending product information in the request body.

### Status Code

201 Created

### Response

The API returns the created product with a newly generated **id**.

Example response:

``` json
{
  "id": 195,
  "title": "BMW Pencil"
}

 ```

### Fields

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | Unique identifier assigned to the newly created product |
| title | Name of the product provided in the request |

### Observation

The API successfully created the product and assigned a new **product ID (195)**. The response includes only the fields that were provided in the request body.

### Endpoint

GET {{base_url}}/products/{{product_id}}

### Description

This endpoint retrieves the details of a specific product using its unique product ID.

### Status Code

200 OK

### Response

The API returns a JSON object containing product information such as id, title, price, rating, stock availability, description, brand, category, and product images.

StartFragment

### Key Fields

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | Unique identifier of the product |
| title | Name of the product |
| price | Price of the product |
| rating | Average product rating |
| stock | Number of products available |
| brand | Product brand |
| category | Product category |
| images | List of product image URLs |
| thumbnail | Product thumbnail image |

### Observation

The API successfully returned the product details for the specified product ID.

EndFragment

StartFragment

### Endpoint

GET {{base_url}}/products?limit=1

### Description

This endpoint retrieves products with a specified limit. The **limit parameter** controls how many products are returned in the response.

### Status Code

200 OK

### Response

The API returns a JSON object containing a **products array** with one product because the limit was set to **1**. Each product contains details such as id, title, description, category, price, rating, stock, brand, reviews, and images.

### Additional Fields

- **total** – total number of products available in the database (194)
    
- **skip** – number of products skipped (pagination)
    
- **limit** – number of products returned in the response
    

### Observation

The API correctly returned **one product** because the limit parameter was set to **1**.

EndFragment

### Endpoint

GET {{base_url}}/products?limit=0

### Description

This endpoint retrieves products with a specified limit. The **limit parameter** controls how many products are returned in the response.

### Status Code

200 OK

### Response

The API returns a JSON object containing a **products array** with one product because the limit was set to 0. Each product contains details such as id, title, description, category, price, rating, stock, brand, reviews, and images.

### Additional Fields

- **total** – total number of products available in the database (194)
    
- **skip** – number of products skipped (pagination)
    
- **limit** – number of products returned in the response
    

### Observation

The API correctly returned **all product** because the limit parameter was set to 0.

exec: pm.test("Response time is less than 2000ms", function () {

request.description:
StartFragmentThe response time for the first request was approximately **1.11 seconds**.

when comparing with the call with a delay this call runs faster

This request with the delay parameter took longer to respond than the request without delay.

The response time increased by approximately **2 seconds**, which matches the **delay=2000 milliseconds** parameter, confirming that the delay parameter works as expected.EndFragment

### Endpoint

POST {{base_url}}/auth/login

### Description

This endpoint authenticates a user using valid login credentials (username and password).

### Status Code

200 OK

### Response

The API returns user information along with authentication tokens used to access protected endpoints.

Example response fields:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| accessToken | Token used to authenticate API requests |
| refreshToken | Token used to generate a new access token |
| id | Unique user identifier |
| username | Username of the authenticated user |
| email | User's email address |
| firstName | User's first name |
| lastName | User's last name |
| gender | User's gender |
| image | Profile image of the user |

### Observation

The API successfully authenticated the user and returned an **access token and refresh token**, which can be used for authorized requests.

### Endpoint

GET {{base_url}}/auth/me

### Description

This endpoint retrieves information about the **currently authenticated user** using a valid access token.

### Status Code

200 OK

### Response

The API returns a JSON object containing detailed information about the authenticated user.

### Key Fields

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | Unique identifier of the user |
| firstName | User's first name |
| lastName | User's last name |
| age | User's age |
| gender | User's gender |
| email | User's email address |
| username | User's login username |
| phone | User's phone number |
| birthDate | User's date of birth |
| image | User profile image |

### Additional Information

The response also includes:

- **Address information**
    
- **Bank details**
    
- **Company information**
    
- **Hair and physical attributes**
    
- **Crypto wallet details**
    
- **User role**
    

### Observation

The API successfully returned detailed profile information for the authenticated user when a valid access token was provided.
